message:
fix(core): drop materialized-CTE plumbing on the attached-only branch of execute_select_via_memdb_fallback so CTE names can't shadow attached-schema tables

When `execute_select_via_memdb_fallback` strips an attached-
schema prefix (e.g. `aux.t` → `t`), it has two execution paths:

  - The query references local tables in addition to the attached
    schema → keep using the memdb fallback (handled in the
    earlier branch).
  - The query references ONLY the attached schema → forward the
    stripped query to the attached connection.

For the attached-only branch we were calling
`self.snapshot_materialized_temp_tables(&temp_tables)?` and
passing the result into the attached connection's
`execute_statement_with_materialized_temp_tables`.  That snapshot
included CTE bindings, which means once the stripped query
reached the attached connection, a CTE named `t` would shadow
an attached table named `t`.  But by the time we strip the
schema prefix, the user's query is `SELECT * FROM t` referring
to `aux.t` — and the CTE shadowing made it resolve to the CTE
binding instead, returning the wrong rows (or zero rows when the
CTE was empty).

Fix: on the attached-only branch, pass an empty
`Vec<MaterializedTempTable>` to the attached connection.  The
stripped query has been proven (by the preceding
`select_references_allowed_local_tables(&stripped, &cte_names) ==
false` check) to reference only the attached schema, so it never
needs the materialized CTEs anyway — and not passing them avoids
the shadowing trap entirely.

Comment block above the `Vec::new()` documents the
"T1.5: stripped query references ONLY the attached schema"
invariant so a future refactor doesn't reintroduce the snapshot
without first re-deriving the proof.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
